Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Xanthomonas campestris causing water-soaked spots that turn brown with yellow halos on leaves.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide. Use disease-free certified seeds. Avoid working in field when wet.

Pepper__bell___healthy.description: No disease detected. Bell pepper plant appears healthy.
Pepper__bell___healthy.treatment: Maintain regular watering. Apply balanced fertilizer. Monitor for pests weekly.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Alternaria solani causing dark brown concentric ring lesions on older potato leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Avoid overhead irrigation. Ensure good soil drainage.

Potato___healthy.description: No disease detected. Potato plant appears healthy.
Potato___healthy.treatment: Maintain consistent watering schedule and hilling. Scout weekly for early disease signs.

Potato___Late_blight.description: Phytophthora infestans causing water-soaked lesions rapidly turning brown-black; highly contagious.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply metalaxyl fungicide immediately. Destroy infected plants entirely. Never leave infected debris in field.

Tomato__Target_Spot.description: Corynespora cassiicola causing brown circular lesions with concentric yellow-brown rings.
Tomato__Target_Spot.treatment: Apply azoxystrobin or difenoconazole. Improve air circulation. Remove lowest leaves as preventive measure.

Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Tomato Mosaic Virus (ToMV) causing mosaic leaf patterns, distortion, and stunted growth.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control aphid vectors with insecticide. Disinfect all tools.

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.description: TYLCV transmitted by whiteflies causing severe leaf curl, yellowing, and significant yield loss.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.treatment: Control whitefly with imidacloprid. Use reflective mulches. Plant resistant varieties where available.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.description: Xanthomonas vesicatoria causing dark water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide weekly. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove infected debris.

Tomato_Early_blight.description: Alternaria solani causing concentric ring 'target-board' lesions on older leaves.
Tomato_Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb every 7–10 days. Remove and destroy lower infected leaves. Rotate crops annually.

Tomato_healthy.description: No disease detected. Tomato plant appears healthy with vibrant green foliage.
Tomato_healthy.treatment: Continue balanced NPK fertilization. Scout weekly for early pest and disease signs.

Tomato_Late_blight.description: Phytophthora infestans — a destructive water mold spreading fast in cool, wet conditions.
Tomato_Late_blight.treatment: Apply metalaxyl + mancozeb immediately. Destroy all infected plant material. Avoid wetting foliage.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.description: Passalora fulva causing olive-brown mold patches on leaf undersides in humid conditions.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.treatment: Reduce humidity below 85%. Improve air circulation. Apply copper-based or chlorothalonil fungicide.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Septoria lycopersici causing small circular spots with grey centers and dark borders.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil. Remove infected leaves immediately. Mulch soil to reduce splash spread.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.description: Tetranychus urticae causing yellowing, stippling, and fine webbing on leaf undersides.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.treatment: Apply miticide or neem oil. Increase ambient humidity. Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiidae).
